programme.title: Total Sport Merseyside: Pre-Match

programme.title: Total Sport Merseyside: Rugby...

programme.desc: Dave Monks presents locally sourced emerging music from the Liverpool City Region.

programme.title: Late Night Jo
programme.desc: Jo Good with late-night conversation, amazing stories and the soundtrack to your night.

programme.title: In Sickness and in Health

programme.title: Man About The House

programme.title: Duty Free

programme.title: Bread

programme.title: Oh Doctor Beeching!

programme.title: Rising Damp

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: When Mildred is away visiting her sister, George attempts to evict the tenants so that he and Jerry can convert the flat into separate units to increase rental income. S3 E6: We Shall Not Be Moved

programme.title: Just Good Friends

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: Mildred is upset that she has not received an invitation to Norman and Chrissy's wedding, but George has hidden it from her because he wants to go to a darts match. S6 E7: Another Bride, Another Groom

programme.title: Some Mothers Do 'Ave 'Em

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry is working in a restaurant and trying to protect Charlie and Christina from a family who will stop at nothing to close it. S1 E10: The Dessert Song

programme.title: On the Buses

programme.title: Rising Damp

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Health and fitness are the new buzzwords in the house when Rigsby's keenness to portray himself in prime condition leads him to challenge Philip to a boxing match. S2 E3: A Body Like Mine

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: When Miss Jones becomes engaged to a librarian and looks set to move out, Rigsby is distraught but is determined that no other man should have her. S2 E4: Moonlight and Roses

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby has found a new lodger called Seymour who is staying in town away from his country estate, but Philip and Alan aren't quite as convinced of his authenticity. S2 E5: A Perfect Gentleman

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby is hoping to take new tenant Brenda out for the evening, but he has a cashflow problem and a string of unwelcome visitors who start arriving at the house. S2 E6: Last of the Big Spenders

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Could rumours of the Grey Lady who haunts the house be true? Rigsby certainly believes so, but he has not seen her yet... S2 E7: Things That Go Bump In The Night

programme.title: Rising Damp Xmas 1975
programme.desc: Classic sitcom about a miserly landlord. Rigsby faces the prospect of a lonely Christmas until Alan and Philip arrive unexpectedly. S2 E8: For the Man Who Has Everything (Christmas Special 1975)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby returns from a Spanish holiday to find Alan has let out the two spare rooms; he's happy Miss Jones has returned but less enthusiastic about a single mother and her baby. S3 E1: That's My Boy

programme.title: Some Mothers Do 'Ave 'Em
programme.desc: Classic sitcom with one-man disaster area Frank Spencer. Frank and Betty go for a spin in his new car and end up having a clifftop picnic. S2 E1: Cliffhanger

programme.title: Some Mothers Do 'Ave 'Em
programme.desc: Classic sitcom about a one-man disaster area. Frank attends an RAF reunion and the hidden story of his short but eventful spell in the services unfolds in flashback. S2 E2: The RAF Reunion

programme.title: Some Mothers Do 'Ave 'Em
programme.desc: Classic sitcom featuring Frank Spencer, a well-meaning one-man disaster area. Betty persuades Frank to take a short course in public relations. S2 E3: The Public Relations Course

programme.title: Some Mothers Do 'Ave 'Em
programme.desc: Classic sitcom. Betty has to break the news to Frank that she is pregnant, and Frank goes for a job at a holiday camp with predictably disastrous results. S2 E4: Frank and Marvin

programme.title: Some Mothers Do 'Ave 'Em
programme.desc: Frank and Betty try to get some practice at looking after children by taking Judy and Alison for a day out at the zoo, and Frank attends a maternity class without Betty. S2 E5: Father's Clinic

programme.title: Some Mothers Do 'Ave 'Em
programme.desc: After a few false alarms, the great day arrives and Betty and Frank's baby is due - Betty wakes one night and Frank must follow his 'routine' to get her to hospital on time. S2 E6: The Baby Arrives

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: Robin and Chrissy have borrowed the Ropers' car to drive to Southampton for an Arsenal match, but the car breaks down and they miss the game. S4 E1: Home & Away

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: Chrissy is practising for her driving test and is receiving lessons from Robin; meanwhile, George has started making home brew liquor which turns out stronger than expected. S4 E2: One for the Road

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: When suspected woodworm is discovered in the upstairs flat, the girls and Robin have to find alternative accommodation while George calls in his so-called handyman. S4 E3: All In The Game

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: Larry gives girls fake names; the most recent one he uses is Robin Tripp, so Chrissy and Jo think Robin is a real bounder when a girl turns up saying she is pregnant. S4 E4: Never Give Your Real Name

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: Robin accompanies Chrissy to her nephew's christening while Jo runs out of excuses not to hear George sing in the British Legion's choir concert. S4 E5: The Tender Trap

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: George has been claiming for a non-existent son for the past 19 years in order to fiddle his tax returns, but he is in danger of being caught when a tax inspector visits. S4 E6: My Son, My Son

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry is not very keen on having to mind a racehorse during a trip to the seaside - that is, until he meets a jockey he takes a shine to. S2 E1: National Pelmet

programme.title: Benidorm
programme.desc: Madge gets a shock when she is reunited with one of her daughters, and the son-in-law she had never heard of. Meanwhile, the Solana is targeted by a con artist and thief, with links to Brandy. S3 E4

programme.title: Shameless
programme.desc: Micky and Aidan come up with a very Shameless take on the Robin Hood story as they rob the rich to give to themselves. But it's not long before the police are involved. S8 E8

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: Robin has failed his cookery exam and considers moving back to Southampton, but the girls are far from pleased at the thought of his mate Larry moving in. S2 E6: Carry Me Back to Old Southampton

programme.title: The Queen's Cavalry
programme.desc: A look behind the scenes at life in the Household Cavalry. Featuring the story of some famous horses of the past and the latest equine recruit, Freddy.

programme.title: Heroes of WWII
programme.desc: The story of Colonel Count Claus von Stauffenberg and the other German officers who tried to kill Adolf Hitler in a bomb plot of July 1944. Ep3: The Man Who Stood Up To Hitler

programme.title: London's East End in WWII
programme.desc: The East End is rocked by the Luftwaffe in 1940 as whole areas are reduced to rubble; it emerges victorious into a new world, but the Docks are closed and the old East End will vanish within 30 years.

programme.title: WWII Fighting Heroes
programme.desc: The moving stories of those who experienced the ferocity and intensity of German bombing as the Luftwaffe targeted the major cities of Britain. Ep4: Fire in Their Hearts

programme.title: WWII: Battles Won and Lost
programme.desc: Battles are almost always about gaining territory; soldiers in the bombed remains of Stalingrad are sentenced to death or held captive. Ep4: Seizing Territory

programme.title: The Nazis: A Warning From History
programme.desc: This episode provides insights into how the Nazis' reign of terror in Poland was characterised by huge population upheavals, chaos, petty squabbles and sheer bloodlust. Ep4: The Wild East

programme.title: Upstairs, Downstairs
programme.desc: It's August 1904; with the family upstairs taking their summer holidays, the staff downstairs have free reign in the house even while all the Bellamys are away. S1 E3: Board Wages

programme.title: When the Boat Comes In
programme.desc: Jack Ford returns in an unexpected style after six years in the US; after a nasty experience with a sadistic roommate, Jack realises that he has reached rock bottom. S4 E1: Back to Dear Old Blighty

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: One of Arthur's business interests is being targeted - Terry gets involved to find out what is going on, but is Arthur's business partner the problem? S1 E8: Come in T-64, Your Time Is Ticking Away

programme.title: You Rang, M'Lord?
programme.desc: Poppy arranges a secret assignation with James, and a fancy dress party is planned to celebrate Cissy's birthday which brings a few surprises. S1 E3 Love or Money

programme.title: Oh Doctor Beeching!
programme.desc: The station is prepared for a visit by the chairman of British Railways, but it turns out to be a different Doctor Beeching altogether. S2 E8: Action Stations

programme.title: Are You Being Served? Again!
programme.desc: Mrs Slocombe finds herself in court for stealing a horse-drawn cart, and Mr Humphries' relationship with Mavis Moulterd continues to develop. S1 E3: Grace & Favour

programme.title: In Sickness and in Health
programme.desc: As Alf's wedding to Mrs Hollingbery approaches, the preparations unfold: present giving, Alf's stag night and finally the big event itself. S5 E10: Wedding

programme.title: Kenny Everett Video Show
programme.desc: With The Boomtown Rats and Suzi Quatro. The legendary BAFTA-winning music and comedy show from Thames TV (1978). All in the best possible taste!

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: When asked to meet at an airport, Simon is asked to help in a mission to stop an international art smuggling ring also involving a former girlfriend of his. S2 E27: The Saint Sees It Through

programme.title: Upstairs, Downstairs
programme.desc: It's May 1905 and Elizabeth is back in London with the house and with her family, but are any of them prepared for her as she is introduced to high society? S1 E4: The Path of Duty

programme.title: When the Boat Comes In
programme.desc: Jack comes back to Gallowshield and is shocked to hear of Matt Headley's death; he feels that Sarah's brother is hiding something and sets out to discover what it is. S4 E2: A Gift From Heaven

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur gets involved with a singer - when he and Terry have a disagreement, he also gets involved with the notorious Vic Piner. Can Terry rescue Arthur? S1 E9: Monday Night Fever

programme.title: You Rang, M'Lord?

programme.title: Oh Doctor Beeching!
programme.desc: It's Jack and May's wedding anniversary, but May is worried that she might be pregnant - meanwhile, Arnold fears that he is about to get the sack. S2 E9: A Pregnant Pause

programme.title: Are You Being Served? Again!
programme.desc: A party of Americans stay at the Millstone Country Hotel for a weekend - it means £1,000 plus tips, so the staff set about bringing the ageing hotel up to scratch. S1 E4: Grace & Favour

programme.title: In Sickness Xmas 1990
programme.desc: 1990 Christmas Special. Alf can't understand why Mrs Hollingbery jilted him and he's hardly full of Xmas spirit!

programme.title: Kenny Everett Video Show
programme.desc: With Elvis Costello and David Essex. The legendary BAFTA-winning music and comedy show from Thames TV (1979). All in the best possible taste! S2 Ep1

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: Chrissy's new boyfriend wants to take her to Bournemouth for the weekend, but Robin discovers something about him that he thinks might make Chrissy want to reconsider. S2 E3: In Praise Of Older Men

programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: Rose finds a very familiar face on board her omnibus and Lady Prudence angers Hazel with her request for a tea party at Eaton Place. S4 E6: Home Fires

programme.title: Bread
programme.desc: Lilo Lill turns up with some sad news for Freddie, Joey rents a shop and Shifty gets a job as a bus driver. S7 E3

programme.title: Steptoe and Son
programme.desc: Harold tries to kill Albert while sleepwalking, leading to a visit to a psychiatrist, triggering memories of when Albert scared away Harold's girlfriend. Featuring Joanna Lumley. S7 E5: Loathe Story

programme.title: Oh Doctor Beeching!
programme.desc: Harry Lambert has been on duty all night in the signal box. The delivery of a body causes problems - should it be classed as freight or passenger? S1 E5: The Late Mr Buckly

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby's a little over-enthusiastic with advice when a couple of newly-weds move into the house. However, a rude surprise awaits him. S4 E1: Hello Young Lovers

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: When Mildred is away visiting her sister, George attempts to evict the tenants so that he and Jerry can convert the flat into separate units to increase the rental income. S3 E6: We Shall Not Be Moved

programme.title: Goodnight Sweetheart
programme.desc: Phoebe's in the present, Yvonne is in the past, and when they meet in the middle, the sparks really begin to fly. S5 E9: Love the One You're With

programme.title: When the Boat Comes In
programme.desc: Thugs beat Jack up. He knows which 'posh fellas' sent them and plans his rervenge. Jessie's husband has a heart to heart with Jack and clears the air. S3 E2: A Tiger, A Lamb & A Basket......of Fruit

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: When Arthur's GP orders him to cut down on booze and cigars, his grip on reality is called into question after he demands police protection from an angry customer. S8 E6: The Last Temptation of Daley

programme.title: Just Good Friends
programme.desc: John Sullivan's bittersweet sitcom. Penny and Vince return from an unexpected rendezvous in Paris to pursue their separate careers. S3 E2: Back In London

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: Mildred is upset that she has not received an invitation to Norman and Chrissy's wedding. But George has hidden it from her because he wants to go to a darts match. S6 E7: Another Bride, Another Groom

programme.title: Some Mothers Do Ave Em
programme.desc: Classic sitcom about the one-man disaster area, Frank Spencer. Frank finds it hard to believe that the council has condemned their old house - especially after all the DIY improvements he made. S3 E1

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Dorien's Book Group is convening to discuss Crime and Punishment - which turns out to be rather applicable to the events that unfold in Tracey's household. S11 E5: Tracey's Choice

programme.desc: Today's news for Greater Manchester from the That's TV Headline News Team

programme.title: Benidorm
programme.desc: Love is in the air, as Geoff meets a mystery woman he's been chatting to online, and Brandy seduces Mateo. Opportunity knocks for Mel, meanwhile, as he searches for the owner of Peacock Island. S3 E3

programme.title: Shameless
programme.desc: Kelly and Shane's new business lands them in trouble when a customer gets more than they bargained for when they try to fulfil their sexual fantasy. S8 E7

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: Jo is going on a date with Philip, but has forgotten that she is meant to be babysitting. Chrissy agrees to take her place and persuades Robin to come with her. S2 E5: Two Foot Two, Eyes Of Blue

programme.title: Rising Damp Finale
programme.desc: With his decree absolute at long last in hand, Rigsby finally pops the question to Miss Jones - but will she accept? S4 E6: Come On In The Water's Lovely

programme.title: Minder

programme.title: In Sickness and in Health
programme.desc: Mrs Hollingbery is forcing Alf to try and save money for their wedding. That includes no smoking and no drinking. S5 E9: Storm

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: The Ropers are invited for a meal, not realising that Robin has invited his German friend Franz. The meal does not go well, as George has a grudge against the Germans. S2 E4: Did You Ever Meet Rommel?

programme.desc: Frank and Betty try to get some practice at looking after children by taking Judy and Alison for a day out at the zoo. And Frank attends a maternity class - without Betty. S2 E5

programme.title: Some Mothers Do Ave Em
programme.desc: After a few false alarms, the great day has arrived, and Betty and Frank's baby is due. Betty wakes during the night, and Frank must follow his 'routine' to get her to hospital on time. S2 E6

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: Robin and Chrissy have borrowed the Ropers car to drive to Southampton for the Arsenal match, but the car breaks down and they miss the game. S4 E1: Home & Away

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: Chrissy is practising for her driving test and is receiving lessons from Robin. Meanwhile, George has started making home brew liquor, which turns out stronger than expected. S4 E2: One For The Road

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: When suspected woodworm is discovered in the upstairs flat, the girls and Robin have to find alternative accommodation whilst George calls in his so-called handyman. S4 E3: All In The Game

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: Larry gives girls fake names, the most recent one he uses is Robin Tripp, so when a girl turns up saying she is pregnant, Chrissy and Jo think Robin is a real bounder. S4 E4: Never Give Your Real Name

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: Robin accompanies Chrissy to her nephew's christening. Jo meanwhile, runs out of excuses not to hear George sing in the British Legion's choir concert. S4 E5: The Tender Trap

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: In order to fiddle his tax returns, George has been claiming for a non-existent son for the past nineteen years but is in danger of being caught when a tax inspector visits. S4 E6: My Son, My Son

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: During a trip to the seaside, Terry is not very keen on having to mind a racehorse - until he meets a jockey he takes a shine to. S2 E1: National Pelmet

programme.title: Benidorm
programme.desc: Madge gets a shock when she is reunited with one of her daughters, and the son-in-law she had never heard of. Meanwhile, the Solana is targeted by a con artist and thief, with links to Brandy. S3 E4

programme.title: Shameless
programme.desc: Micky and Aidan come up with a very Shameless take on the Robin Hood story as they rob the rich to give to themselves. But it's not long before the police are involved. S8 E8

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: Robin has failed his cookery exam and considers moving back to Southampton. But the girls are far from pleased at the thought of his mate Larry moving in. S2 E6: Carry Me Back To Old Southampton

programme.desc: Mr Jeremy Brown, an English teacher, meets his class of foreign students for the first time. Things start off well, but... S1 E1: The First Lesson

programme.title: Minder

programme.desc: Chrissy's new boyfriend wants to take her to Bournemouth for the weekend, but Robin discovers something about him that he thinks might make Chrissy want to reconsider. S2 E3: In Praise Of Older Men

programme.desc: Rose finds a very familiar face on board her omnibus and Lady Prudence angers Hazel with her request for a tea party at Eaton Place. S4 E6: Home Fires

programme.title: Bread
programme.desc: Lilo Lill turns up with some sad news for Freddie, Joey rents a shop and Shifty gets a job as a bus driver. S7 E3

programme.title: Steptoe and Son
programme.desc: Harold tries to kill Albert while sleepwalking, leading to a visit to a psychiatrist, triggering memories of when Albert scared away Harold's girlfriend. Featuring Joanna Lumley. S7 E5: Loathe Story

programme.title: Oh Doctor Beeching!
programme.desc: Harry Lambert has been on duty all night in the signal box. The delivery of a body causes problems - should it be classed as freight or passenger? S1 E5: The Late Mr Buckly

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby's a little over-enthusiastic with advice when a couple of newly-weds move into the house. However, a rude surprise awaits him. S4 E1: Hello Young Lovers

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: When Mildred is away visiting her sister, George attempts to evict the tenants so that he and Jerry can convert the flat into separate units to increase the rental income. S3 E6: We Shall Not Be Moved

programme.title: Goodnight Sweetheart
programme.desc: Phoebe's in the present, Yvonne is in the past, and when they meet in the middle, the sparks really begin to fly. S5 E9: Love the One You're With

programme.title: When the Boat Comes In
programme.desc: Thugs beat Jack up. He knows which 'posh fellas' sent them and plans his rervenge. Jessie's husband has a heart to heart with Jack and clears the air. S3 E2: A Tiger, A Lamb & A Basket......of Fruit

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: When Arthur's GP orders him to cut down on booze and cigars, his grip on reality is called into question after he demands police protection from an angry customer. S8 E6: The Last Temptation of Daley

programme.title: Just Good Friends
programme.desc: John Sullivan's bittersweet sitcom. Penny and Vince return from an unexpected rendezvous in Paris to pursue their separate careers. S3 E2: Back In London

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: Mildred is upset that she has not received an invitation to Norman and Chrissy's wedding. But George has hidden it from her because he wants to go to a darts match. S6 E7: Another Bride, Another Groom

programme.title: Some Mothers Do Ave Em
programme.desc: Classic sitcom about the one-man disaster area, Frank Spencer. Frank finds it hard to believe that the council has condemned their old house - especially after all the DIY improvements he made. S3 E1

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Dorien's Book Group is convening to discuss Crime and Punishment - which turns out to be rather applicable to the events that unfold in Tracey's household. S11 E5: Tracey's Choice

programme.desc: Kelly and Shane's new business lands them in trouble when a customer gets more than they bargained for when they try to fulfil their sexual fantasy. S8 E7

programme.desc: Phoebe's in the present, Yvonne is in the past, and when they meet in the middle, the sparks really begin to fly. S5 E9: Love the One You're With

programme.title: When the Boat Comes In
programme.desc: Thugs beat Jack up. He knows which 'posh fellas' sent them and plans his rervenge. Jessie's husband has a heart to heart with Jack and clears the air. S3 E2: A Tiger, A Lamb & A Basket......of Fruit

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: When Arthur's GP orders him to cut down on booze and cigars, his grip on reality is called into question after he demands police protection from an angry customer. S8 E6: The Last Temptation of Daley

programme.title: Just Good Friends
programme.desc: John Sullivan's bittersweet sitcom. Penny and Vince return from an unexpected rendezvous in Paris to pursue their separate careers. S3 E2: Back In London

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: Mildred is upset that she has not received an invitation to Norman and Chrissy's wedding. But George has hidden it from her because he wants to go to a darts match. S6 E7: Another Bride, Another Groom

programme.title: Collapse The Box
programme.desc: Noelle McAlinden discusses the highlights of the stunning annual Royal Ulster Academy exhibition with arts expert Eimear O'Connor.

programme.title: Benidorm
programme.desc: Love is in the air, as Geoff meets a mystery woman he's been chatting to online, and Brandy seduces Mateo. Opportunity knocks for Mel, meanwhile, as he searches for the owner of Peacock Island. S3 E3

programme.title: Shameless
programme.desc: Kelly and Shane's new business lands them in trouble when a customer gets more than they bargained for when they try to fulfil their sexual fantasy. S8 E7

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: With Miss Jones collecting for a famine charity, Philip challenges Rigsby to go 48 hours without eating. He gladly accepts the offer, but soon finds his will weakening. S2 E2: Food Glorious Food

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Health and fitness are the new buzzwords in the house, and Rigsby's keenness to portray himself as in prime condition lead him to challenging Philip to a boxing match. S2 E3: A Body Like Mine

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Miss Jones has become engaged to a librarian and looks set to move out - Rigsby is distraught but determined that no other man should have her. S2 E4: Moonlight and Roses

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby's found a new lodger called Seymour, who's staying in town away from his country estate. Philip and Alan aren't quite as convinced of his authenticity though. S2 E5: A Perfect Gentleman

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby's hoping to take new tenant Brenda out for the evening. Unfortunately, he has a cashflow problem, and a string of unwelcome visitors start arriving at the house. S2 E6: Last of the Big Spenders

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Could rumours of the Grey Lady who haunts the house be true? Rigsby certainly believes so, although he has not seen her - yet. S2 E7: Things That Go Bump In The Night

programme.title: Rising Damp Xmas 1975
programme.desc: Rigsby is facing the prospect of a lonely Christmas, until Alan and Philip arrive unexpectedly. Classic sitcom about a miserly landlord. S2 E8: For the Man Who Has Everything (Christmas Special 1975)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby returns from a Spanish holiday to find Alan has let out the two spare rooms. He's happy Miss Jones has returned, but less enthusiastic about a single mother and her baby. S3 E1: That's My Boy

programme.title: Some Mothers Do Ave Em
programme.desc: Classic sitcom with one-man disaster area Frank Spencer. Frank and Betty go for a spin in his new car and end up having a clifftop picnic. S2 E1

programme.title: Some Mothers Do Ave Em
programme.desc: Classic sitcom about a one-man disaster area. Frank attends an RAF reunion and the hidden story of his short but eventful spell in the services unfolds in flashback. S2 E2

programme.title: Some Mothers Do Ave Em
programme.desc: Classic sitcom featuring Frank Spencer - a well-meaning, one-man disaster area. Betty persuades Frank to take a short course in public relations. S2 E3

programme.title: Some Mothers Do Ave Em
programme.desc: Classic sitcom. Betty has to break the news to Frank that she is pregnant. And Frank goes for a job at a holiday camp, with predictably disastrous results. S2 E4

programme.title: Some Mothers Do Ave Em
programme.desc: Frank and Betty try to get some practice at looking after children by taking Judy and Alison for a day out at the zoo. And Frank attends a maternity class - without Betty. S2 E5

programme.title: Some Mothers Do Ave Em
programme.desc: After a few false alarms, the great day has arrived, and Betty and Frank's baby is due. Betty wakes during the night, and Frank must follow his 'routine' to get her to hospital on time. S2 E6

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: Robin and Chrissy have borrowed the Ropers car to drive to Southampton for the Arsenal match, but the car breaks down and they miss the game. S4 E1: Home & Away

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: Chrissy is practising for her driving test and is receiving lessons from Robin. Meanwhile, George has started making home brew liquor, which turns out stronger than expected. S4 E2: One For The Road

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: When suspected woodworm is discovered in the upstairs flat, the girls and Robin have to find alternative accommodation whilst George calls in his so-called handyman. S4 E3: All In The Game

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: Larry gives girls fake names, the most recent one he uses is Robin Tripp, so when a girl turns up saying she is pregnant, Chrissy and Jo think Robin is a real bounder. S4 E4: Never Give Your Real Name

programme.title: Man About The House
programme.desc: Robin accompanies Chrissy to her nephew's christening. Jo meanwhile, runs out of excuses not to hear George sing in the British Legion's choir concert. S4 E5: The Tender Trap

programme.title: Man About The House

programme.title: Minder

programme.desc: Madge gets a shock when she is reunited with one of her daughters, and the son-in-law she had never heard of. Meanwhile, the Solana is targeted by a con artist and thief, with links to Brandy. S3 E4

programme.title: Shameless
programme.desc: Micky and Aidan come up with a very Shameless take on the Robin Hood story as they rob the rich to give to themselves. But it's not long before the police are involved. S8 E8

programme.title: Seo BBC ALBA. This is BBC ALBA
programme.desc: Sealladh ùr bho Alba. A new view from Scotland.

programme.title: Na Clangairean
programme.desc: Tha Meanbh is Beag ag adhbharachadh tubaist a tha a’ gabhail a-steach dìtheanan bulgach, am Moo-adhair, agus a’ Chearc Iarainn. Ciamar a thèid cùisean a cheartachadh? Meanbh and Beag cause an accident which involves bubble flowers, the Sky-Moo and the Iron Chicken. How will they get things sorted?

programme.title: Meaban and Moo
programme.desc: Bidh Linda a’ tadhal air Meaban is Moo gus dùbhlain spòrsail a chur romhpa. Anns a’ phrògram seo tha i ag iarraidh orra trì rudan dubha a lorg. Tha Moo cuideachd airson brot a dhèanamh agus tha Meaban rudeigin troimh-chèile le na th’ aca ri dhèanamh! Linda visits Meaban and Moo to set them fun challenges. In this programme, she tasks them with finding three black items. Moo also wants to make soup and Meaban is rather flustered with all that has to be done!

programme.title: Creag nam Buthaidean/Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Marvin is having a bad, bad day - he has lost his favourite rock. Oona tries to help him look for a new one, but all the new rocks are too small, too heavy, or too pointy. It seems no other rock will do. But when Oona spots that the seagulls have Marvin's old rock, she comes up with a plan to help him get it back! Tha droch, droch là aig Marvin- e air a' chreag as fheàrr leis a chall. Tha Oona ga chuideachadh gus tè ùr fhaighinn, ach tha gach creag ùr ro bheag, ro throm no ro bhiorach. E mar nach dèan creag eile a' chùis. Ach nuair a chì Oona g' eil seann chreag Marvin aig na faoileagan, tha beachd aice air mar a gheibh i air ais i dha-sa

programme.title: Creag nam Buthaidean/Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Mama and Papa are taking Oona and Baba on a family outing to experience something special - Papa tells them it's a big surprise. Oona and Baba are very excited to see it, but they need to find the perfect spot - which might not be so easy. In the end, though, the amazing surprise makes all of their effort worth it! Tha Mamaidh is Papaidh a' toirt Oona is Baba air cuairt teaghlaich gus rudeigin sònraicht' fhoghlam – tha Papaidh ag ràdh riuth' gur h-e iongnadh MÒR a th' ann. Tha Oona is Baba air bhioran 'son fhaicinn, ach feumaidh 'ad an t-àit' as fheàrr a lorg – rud nach bi cho soirbh 's dòch'.Ach aig a' cheann thall, tha an t-iongnadh mìorbhaileach airidh air an cuid saothair!

programme.title: Peicein/Petit
programme.desc: Tha Peicein is a charaidean gann de dh’airgead, is leis nach eil ann an airgead ach pìosan beaga pàipeir le dealbhan orra, tha iad uile a’ co-dhùnadh gun cruthaich iad an t-airgead aca fhèin, is gun ceannaich iad na thogras iad. Because they are a little short of money, and considering money notes are only little papers with drawings on them, Petit and his friends decide to create their own money and buy anything they want.

programme.title: Sionnach agus Maigheach (Fox & Hare)
programme.desc: Tha Feòrag a’ stèidheachadh club nam ban agus a’ toirt cuireadh gu greim bìdh dhan h-uile leadaidh. Tha Sionnach is Tosg a’ feuchainn ri faighinn a-steach le bhith gan sgeadachadh fhèin mar leadaidhean, ach nuair a thuigeas Feòrag cò iad, tha an dithis a’ faighinn a’ bhròig. Tha an tàmailt a’ toirt air Sionnach is Tosg smaoineachadh mu chlub nam fear a chur air chois, ach saoil an aontaich iad air cur-seachad a chòrdas riutha uile? Brush decides to start a ladies club and invites all the ladies of the forest for brunch. When Fox and Tusk try to join in, dressed as ladies, Brush soon discovers their true identity and kicks them out. Offended, Fox and Tusk decide to start a men’s club. But can they come up with a club activity that everyone likes?

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Sgeulachdan do chlann òga. Tha Art MacCarmaig a’ leughadh Alfons, chan fhaod thu sin a dhèanamh! Chaidh an leabhar a sgrìobhadh ‘s a dhealbhachadh le Daisy Hirst agus chaidh eadar-theangachadh dhan Ghàidhlig le Dòmhnall Iain MacLeòid. A bedtime story for young children. Arthur Cormack reads Alfons, chan fhaod thu sin a dhèanamh! The book was written and illustrated by Daisy Hirst and translated into Gaelic by Donald John MacLeod.

programme.title: An Teaghlach Rìoghail an Ath-dhoras/The Royal Next Door
programme.desc: Tha Stella 'g iarraidh a bhith mar dhaoine eile ach tha beachd eile aig Màthair na Bànrigh! Tha ise airson dèanamh cinnteach nach eil Stella àbhaisteach. Le sin, tha i airson Clasaichean Rìoghail ullachadh dhi. Stella wants to be like everyone else, but the Queen Mother has other ideas. She would like to ensure that Stella isn’t normal, so arranges royal classes for her.

programme.title: An Teaghlach Rìoghail an Ath-dhoras/The Royal Next Door
programme.desc: Tha Stella air màirneal fhaighinn airson a’ chiad turas a-riamh – an dèidh mi-thuigs’ mu thilgeil ball-pàipear san tional. Chan fhaod a parantan faighinn a-mach mun seo. Ciamar a thèid dhi? Stella has been given detention for the first time ever, after a misunderstanding over the throwing of a paper ball in assembly. Her parents must not find out about this. How will she get on?

programme.title: Clann Na Cruinne
programme.desc: Nuair a b’ fheudar dha teàghlach Kerene gluasad bhon Chongo dhan Ghrèig, cha robh Greugais aice. Ach, le taic bho a caraid, Aggeliki, tha i a-nis fileanta. Tha dannsadh uabhasach cudromach dhi is tha i airson buidheann dannsaidh a thòiseachadh. When Kerene’s family had to move from the Congo to Greece, she couldn’t speak Greek. But with the help of her friend Aggeliki, she is now fluent. Dancing is very important to her and she wants to set up a dance group.

programme.title: Belle and Sebastian
programme.desc: Tha muinntir an àite air a bhith ri lorg Àradh na h-Aimheis fad linntean – gun sgeul sam bith - agus tha Sonny agus Sebastian dol a dh’ fheuchainn ri lorg cuideachd. Saoil am bi iad soirbheachail? The locals have been looking for the Ladder of Void for generations, without any success, and Sonny and Sebastian are also going to search for it. Will they be successful?

programme.title: Seonaidh | Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Tha Bitzer a' toirt cothrom dhan tuathanach ithe ann an àrainneachd thaigh-bìdh, le paidh blasta airson a' phrìomh-chùrsa. Ach nuair a thèid am paidh air chall, tha Bitzer a' dèanamh mearachd ann a bhith a' gabhail taic bho Sheonaidh 's an treud anns a' chidsin. Bitzer treats the farmer to a restaurant-style dining experience, offering a delicious pie as the main course. However when the pie goes missing, Bitzer makes the mistake of accepting Shaun and the flock's offer of help in the kitchen.

programme.title: An Là
programme.desc: Naidheachdan ionadail, nàiseanta is eadar-nàiseanta gach là cuide ri fiosrachadh coimhearsnachd bho BBC ALBA. Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA.

programme.title: Scottish Women's Premier League (BBC ALBA)
programme.desc: Ball-coise beò bhon SWPL le Partick Thistle a’ cur fàilte air Glasgow City. An geama slàn còmhla ri Micheal Steele agus Ailig O’Henley. Live football action from the SWPL as Partick Thistle host Glasgow City. Full match coverage in the company of presenter Micheal Steele and commentator Alex O’Henley.

programme.title: Brunch le Abigail
programme.desc: Waffles - tha iad a’ còrdadh ris a’ mhòr-chuid de dhaoine. Tha Abigail gan dèanamh i fhèin agus gan sgeadachadh gu brèagha. Brunch gu math blasta a dh’fhàgas gu math làn thu. Waffles - most people love them. Abigail makes them from scratch and presents them beautifully. A very tasty and filling brunch. In Gaelic with English subtitles.

programme.title: Steelmen
programme.desc: Ann an 1991, bha baile Thobar na Màthar a’ greimeachadh ris a’ bhuaidh shòisealta, eaconamach agus chultarail a bheireadh dùnadh ionad stàilinn Ravenscraig air an sgìre, ach airson a’ chiad uair ann an 39 bliadhna thog sgioba ball-coise a’ bhaile, na ‘Steelmen' mar a theirear riutha, Cupa na h-Alba, a’ toirt boillsgeadh dòchais a thogadh an dorchadas. In 1991, as the town of Motherwell faced the crippling social, economic and cultural impact of the closure of the Ravenscraig steelworks, its football club, locally known as the Steelmen, lifted the prestigious Scottish Cup for the first time in 39 years. The victory was a glimmer of light in the ensuing darkness.

programme.title: Samhradh air Sàl
programme.desc: Katie MacRitchie from Harris joins Angus Mackay to go fishing on a moody, misty summer's morning off the east coast of Lewis. Watched closely by a golden eagle, they fish for mackerel as the sun gradually emerges and chat about the role that fishing plays in the community life of the islands. Tha Katie NicRisnidh às na Hearadh còmhla ri Aonghas MacAoidh agus iad ag iasgach air madainn shamhraidh ghruamach, cheòthach far costa an ear Leòdhais. Tha iad ag iasgach airson rionnach, le iolaire-bhuidhe gan coimhead, fhad ’s a nochdas a’ ghrian mean air mhean agus iad a’ bruidhinn mun àite a th’ aig iasgach ann am beatha coimhearsnachd nan h-eileanan.

programme.title: Seirm
programme.desc: Prògram de cheòl traidiseanta agus freumhach air a lìbhrigeadh le Màiri Anna NicUalraig. Air fhilmeadh aig àm Celtic Connections 2025 agus a’ taisbeanaidh chlàraidhean is chòmhraidhean a chaidh a dhèanamh a dh’aona ghothach airson Seirm, chìte cuid den luchd-ciùil a b’ fheàrr a bha aig an fhèis, leithid Kíla, Väsen, Gaïa agus Aonghas & Coinneach MacCoinnich le Mac Morin. Traditional and roots music show presented by Mary Ann Kennedy. Filmed during Celtic Connections 2025, Seirm showcases bespoke recordings and conversations with some of the best artists appearing at the festival. This episode features performances from Kíla, Väsen, Gaïa and Angus & Kenneth MacKenzie with Mac Morin. In Gaelic with English subtitles.

programme.title: Seo BBC ALBA. This is BBC ALBA
programme.desc: Sealladh ùr bho Alba. A new view from Scotland.

programme.title: Seo BBC ALBA. This is BBC ALBA
programme.desc: Sealladh ùr bho Alba. A new view from Scotland.

programme.title: Somhairle Spins
programme.desc: Tha Somhairle agus a charaidean bho’n oilthigh a’ deanamh air an t-slighe baidhsagal ainmeil Na Còig Aiseagan, le measgachadh spòrsail de dh’eòlasan, casan sgìth agus tòinean goirt! Somhairle and his university friends take on the infamous Five Ferries cycle route, with an entertaining mix of experience, resulting in tired legs and sore backsides!

programme.title: Rugbaidh air BBC ALBA/Rugby on BBC ALBA
programme.desc: Live rugby coverage of the men’s Scottish Cup final as Currie Chieftains go up against Ayr RFC at Murrayfield. Rugbaidh beò bho Cuairt-Dheireannach Cupa na h-Alba le Currie Chieftains a’ dol an aghaidh Ayr RFC.

programme.title: Brunch le Abigail
programme.desc: Waffles - tha iad a’ còrdadh ris a’ mhòr-chuid de dhaoine. Tha Abigail gan dèanamh i fhèin agus gan sgeadachadh gu brèagha. Brunch gu math blasta a dh’fhàgas gu math làn thu. Waffles - most people love them. Abigail makes them from scratch and presents them beautifully. A very tasty and filling brunch. In Gaelic with English subtitles.

programme.title: An Là
programme.desc: Naidheachdan ionadail, nàiseanta is eadar-nàiseanta gach là cuide ri fiosrachadh coimhearsnachd bho BBC ALBA. Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA.

programme.title: Rugbaidh air BBC ALBA/Rugby on BBC ALBA
programme.desc: Live rugby coverage of the Sarah Beaney Cup final as Watsonians take on Hillhead Jordanhill at Murrayfield. Rugbaidh beò bho Cuairt-Dheireannach Cupa Sarah Beaney le Watsonians a’ dol an aghaidh Hilllhead Jordanhill.

programme.title: Samhradh air Sàl
programme.desc: Angus is joined by long time friend Angus Macmillan as they go fishing off the west coast of Lewis. Leaving Loch Odhairn, they travel south to the lighthouse at the Miolaid where a successful day’s fishing awaits. Against the backdrop of the magical Shiant Islands, they discuss the old-school traditions of island fishing before proving the effectiveness of modern methods, too. Tha deagh charaid Aonghais, Aonghas MacMhaolain, còmhla ris agus iad a’ dol a dh’iasgach far costa siar Leòdhais. A’ fàgail Loch Odhairn, tha iad a’ siubhal gu deas chun taigh-sholais aig a’ Mhiolaid, far a bheil latha soirbheachail air thoiseach orra. Air beulaibh nan Eileanan Mòra, bruidhnidh iad air traidiseanan iasgaich seann-fhasanta nan eilean, mus dearbh iad cho èifeachdach ’s a tha dòighean an latha an-diugh.

programme.title: Immigration Tracks
programme.desc: From Canada's Eastern province of Cape Breton, we embark on a road-trip east to Toronto, Ontario, in search of Scottish Gaelic families who made a new life in this vast country over 100 years ago. This week we experience the European flavour of Quebec City. Anne follows the St Lawrence River to Montreal to meet Neil Mackay, Canada’s oldest Gael. How do generations of Canadian Scots identify with their roots? Join Anne McAlpine on a cultural voyage of discovery. An t-seachdains, tha sinn ann am baile mòr Qhuebec far a bheil faireachdainn Eòrpach. Agus, tha Anne a’ leantainn abhainn an Naoimh Lawrence a Mhontreal, a choinneachadh ris a’ Ghàidheal as sine an Canada – Niall MacAoidh.

programme.title: Seirm
programme.desc: Prògram-ciùil air a lìbhrigeadh le Màiri Anna NicUalraig. Air fhilmeadh aig àm Celtic Connections 2025 agus a’ taisbeanaidh chlàraidhean is chòmhraidhean a chaidh a dhèanamh a dh’aona ghothach airson Seirm, chìte cuid den luchd-ciùil a b’ fheàrr a bha aig an fhèis, leithid Loudon Wainwright III, Ímar, Larry Campbell & Teresa Williams, Ablaye Cissoko & Cyrille Brotto agus Fell Line. Traditional and roots music show presented by Mary Ann Kennedy. Filmed during Celtic Connections 2025, Seirm showcases bespoke recordings and conversations with some of the best artists appearing at the festival. This episode features performances from Loudon Wainwright III, Ímar, Larry Campbell & Teresa Williams, Ablaye Cissoko & Cyrille Brotto and Fell Line. In Gaelic with English subtitles.

programme.title: Fàsach na h-Eòrpa/Europe’s Wilderness
programme.desc: Creutair tearc a bhuineas do bheanntan na Mara Mheadhan-thìrich: 's e eileanan Corsica, Sardinia is Cìopras dachaidh thùsail a' chaora mhouflon A look at the mouflon sheep, originally native to Corsica, Sardinia and Cyprus and a rare inhabitant of the Mediterranean mountains. Dubbed in Gaelic with English subtitles.

programme.title: Hoolie
programme.desc: An oidhche as motha ann an Alba airson ceòl traidiseanta, tha Hoolie 2025 a’ comharrachadh Sir Billy Connolly, gaisgeach Ghlaschu ann an coimeadaidh is ceòl folk. Le ceòl bho rionnagan ann an saoghal beothail ceòl traidiseanta is folk na h-Alba, a leithid Beluga Lagoon, Trail West, Elephant Sessions is Mànran, cuide ri tachartas ceòlmhor sònraichte don Big Yin gus crìoch a chuir air an oidhche. Scotland’s biggest night of traditional music, Hoolie 2025 celebrates Glasgow comedy and folk music legend Sir Billy Connolly. Featuring performances from a stellar line-up across Scotland’s vibrant folk and traditional music scene including Beluga Lagoon, Trail West, Elephant Sessions and Mànran - followed by the finale of the event, a musical tribute to The Big Yin.

programme.title: Belladrum
programme.desc: Tha Fiona NicChoinnich agus Niall Iain Dòmhnallach a’ cur fàilt’ air Skipinnish gu Àrd-Ùrlar Hot House. Freumhaichte ann an ceòl bho thaobh siar na h-Alba, chomharraich an còmhlan 25 bliadhna còmhla le cuirm shònraichte aig Caisteal Dhùn Èidinn an-uiridh agus le taisbeanadh sgoinneil eile an seo, bha an luchd-leantainn a’ seinn ’s a’ dannsa a-rithist air latha 2 de Belladrum 2025. Fiona MacKenzie and Niall Iain Macdonald welcome Skipinnish to the Hot House Stage. Rooted in Scotland’s west coast music scene, the band celebrated 25 years with a spectacular show at Edinburgh Castle last year. Another rousing performance had their fans singing and dancing all over again on day two of Belladrum.

programme.title: Scotland: Rome's Final Frontier
programme.desc: Were the ancient Scottish tribes too much for the Roman Empire? Or was Scotland simply not worth conquering? Archaeologist Dr Fraser Hunter looks back on three centuries of contact and conflict with Scotland’s Roman invaders. The first Tay Bridge, the first depiction of tartan and forgotten Roman camps that once held thirty-five thousand men. A story of a superpower pitted against tribesmen and warlords, and one with fascinating modern parallels.

programme.title: Shark
programme.desc: A look at how an international team of scientists is trying to change people's perceptions of sharks. Their research is revealing that sharks can be sociable and intelligent, and they could even help solve some of the toughest medical challenges of the 21st century. However, the breakthroughs come at a time when many sharks face extinction. Science may now be the only way to save them.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gary Davies presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 23 April 1987, featuring Five Star, The Smiths, Kim Wilde & Junior, Judy Boucher, Starship, Tom Jones, Glenn & Chris, Madonna and Terence Trent D'Arby.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jakki Brambles presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 19 April 1990 and featuring Sonia, Faith No More and Adamski.

programme.title: Roy Orbison at the BBC
programme.desc: A deep dive into the BBC’s archives to celebrate the great Roy Orbison – full-time rock 'n’ roll legend, part-time Traveling Wilbury and the man Elvis famously described as ‘the best singer in the world’. The Big O’s story was in many ways a sad one, full of family tragedies and heartbreak, and his extraordinary voice captured all those emotions and more, bringing a new dimension to popular music. This programme features some of the hits that had fans enraptured and fellow music superstars singing his praises, with performances from his many visits to the UK including Only the Lonely, Crying and Oh Pretty Woman.

programme.title: Roy Orbison and Friends: A Black and White Night
programme.desc: This TV concert classic features Roy Orbison performing his greatest hits with friends Bruce Springsteen, Tom Waits, Elvis Costello, Jackson Browne, kd lang, Jennifer Warnes and Bonnie Raitt. The TCB Band, which backs all the featured artists, was Elvis Presley's band until his death in 1977 and includes James Burton, Glen D Hardin, Jerry Scheff and Ronnie Tutt, with musical director T Bone Burnett. Filmed at the Coconut Grove nightclub in Los Angeles, in black and white (hence the title!), the show was first broadcast on HBO in 1988, the year of Roy Orbison's death.

programme.title: Roy Sings Orbison
programme.desc: Roy Orbison sings some of his greatest hits, including Only the Lonely, Crying, Penny Arcade, Blue Bayou, Running Scared, Candy Man, In Dreams, Mean Woman Blues, It's Over and Oh, Pretty Woman.

programme.title: Top of the Pops

programme.title: Gold Run
programme.desc: April 9, 1940. The German war machine occupies Norway. One of their primary objectives is to seize the nation’s entire gold reserves. But thanks to a few courageous individuals, the gold is smuggled out - just as the Germans come.​​ ​​This is the incredible true story of how 55 tonnes of gold was transported through rough winter landscapes on trucks, trains and boats​ by a group of unlikely resistance fighters ahead of the invading Nazi forces.​

programme.title: Timewatch
programme.desc: Escapologist Harry Houdini was one of the icons of the early 20th century. Restored archive footage and interviews with historians and illusionists recall his extraordinary impact on audiences and reveal the secrets behind some of his most amazing escapes.

programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: The Artemis II mission has been presented as a major milestone in human spaceflight, with global audiences following live coverage and access provided by NASA. Questions have been raised about how official feeds, astronaut storytelling and media partnerships shaped reporting. Rebecca Morelle, BBC News science editor, describes the reporting environment she experienced. The BBC has announced plans to cut around 2,000 jobs and save £500 million, citing financial pressures including declining licence fee income and rising production costs. The changes come ahead of charter renewal negotiations and increased competition. Rhodri Talfan Davies, the BBC’s acting director-general, outlines the scale of the challenge. A Kuwaiti-American journalist is reportedly being held by authorities in Kuwait after posting footage of a US Air Force jet crash near Kuwait City. The case has drawn attention to press freedom in the region. Jodie Ginsberg, CEO of the Committee to Protect Journalists, sets out the known details.

programme.title: Tech Now
programme.desc: Alasdair Keane visits the underground insect farm turning food waste into animal feed. Shiona McCallum takes a tour behind the scenes of Blenheim Palace to see how new technologies are playing a key role in the restoration of the Unesco World Heritage. Yasmin Morgan-Griffiths explores how research linking climate change and extreme weather events is being used in lawsuits against major polluters. And Martin Sharkey takes a journey into the history of the emoji.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: Cook and food writer Rachel Khoo visits the cool of Sweden's capital city to continue her culinary journey. Rachel visits one of Stockholm's hippest restaurants, where rump of deer is on the menu. Back home, she's inspired by Sweden's traditional cinnamon buns and bakes her own savoury Swedish buns as well as cooking venison steak with celeriac puree, pickled blackberries and carrot petals and a lingonberry and raspberry marzipan tart, inspired by the Swedish lingonberry.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Scams & Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims, giving them fake cover stories. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Travel Show
programme.desc: Rob Bell heads to North America's largest ski resort as it fires up its lifts for its 60th season. Whistler Blackcomb has become a bucket list destination for skiers, so Rob - who used to work as a 'liftie' there in his youth - goes behind the scenes to find out what goes into such a mammoth operation. He gets back to work on the lifts, hears the indigenous reaction to having a giant of tourism arrive on their land and spends time with the ski patrollers working to keep the customers safe.

programme.title: Talking Movies
programme.desc: For Talking Movies presenter Tom Brook, April 2026 marks a momentous milestone. It will be the 50th anniversary of Tom joining the BBC as a trainee news journalist. Over the decades, from London, Berlin and mostly New York, he has reported on culture and cinema in thousands of stories and programmes broadcast on the BBC. Tom has interviewed countless leading figures in cinema, from early pioneers like Bette Davis and Jimmy Cagney up to contemporary star names, including Timothee Chalamet, Meryl Streep and Lady Gaga. Tom has also met US presidents and UK prime ministers, and leaders in global culture from Bollywood to LA. In a special edition of Talking Movies, he looks back at his career and the actors and filmmakers he has encountered over 50 years. He reflects on how the film industry has changed over the last five decades and how, in very tumultuous times, he believes cinema is more relevant and useful to our lives than ever before.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: In a case of mistaken identity, a man is stabbed to death. An intelligence analyst helps convict the killers, and a trace evidence expert helps find the weapon used to kill a teenager.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Some 1960s newspapers are uncovered by Martin in Hampshire, Martel is impressed with what’s on offer in Yorkshire, and Tommy feels at home in east London.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A football match between British and German troops on Christmas Day in 1914 was one of the most enduring and symbolic moments of the First World War. In this touching episode, Danny Sebastian hears how a young goalkeeper from Newark took part in that iconic match and may have provided the match ball – a gift from his brother back home. Kicking off their hunt for three profitable antiques are friends and aviation engineers Hannah and Shelly, who reckon they will be shopping to the last minute. Their opponents in blue are dad and daughter Paul and Lorraine, who predict that there will be tears if they don’t win. Straight after the whistle, the reds spot a set of military style storage boxes and a collection of sweetheart brooches, but expert Roo Irvine doesn’t think either will woo the bidders. Meanwhile, the blues are reflecting on an antique hand mirror and some mis-matched metal armour, which is going for an arm and a leg. In Derby, auctioneer James Lewis tries to hammer out profits, and for both teams, there are shiny bonus buys to tempt them.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Brenda finds love at the home of a clock collector, but has she fallen for a killer?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Briony May Williams is with a couple from London in the market for a new home in the countryside of Oxfordshire or Wiltshire that has enough space for them and their two young sons. Taking time away from house hunting, Briony learns why the relatively new practice of not mowing the grass in the month of May is here to stay.

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Looking to change their lives, with the help of Dom and his team of mentors, are woodworker Neil and embroiderer Catherine. After retiring, former police officer Neil wants to build a successful woodworking business, but so far, the orders haven’t been coming in. Mentor Bruce thinks he’s doing too much and needs to create a core range to have a chance at success. When her father passed away, Catherine decided to make her embroidery business dreams a reality, but she hasn’t been able to earn a living from it. Her mentor Stephanie thinks she has great potential but is spreading herself too thin and needs to streamline her work. Both are given eight weeks to follow their mentors’ advice, but can they create successful, sustainable businesses?

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp, with sidekick Sarah Greene, hosts the quiz show where contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor. Correct answers get the players moving, while wrong answers will stop them in their tracks. Five contestants – Justin, Dean, Serena, Joe and Andrea – battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple – make it to the finish line before your rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the £5,000 cash prize.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: Channel Islands News
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather for the Channel Islands.

programme.title: Party Election Broadcasts: Labour
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by the Labour Party for the local and mayoral elections on 7 May.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The stars of The Devil Wears Prada 2 - Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway, Emily Blunt, Stanley Tucci and Simone Ashley - join Lauren Laverne and Angellica Bell on the sofa.

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.desc: On her very first day running her local Post Office, Seema discovered a cash shortfall at the till. She had to put in her own money to make the books add up. Every day, more and more money appeared to be missing. She didn't know it then, but she was the latest victim of the infamous Post Office Horizon accounting scandal, which saw hundreds of sub-postmasters and sub-postmistresses falsely convicted of theft, fraud and false accounting, based on faulty computer data. Desperate to keep her business afloat, Seema even sold her gold wedding ring in an attempt to meet the shortfall. Following a trial, she was convicted and sent to prison whilst pregnant. Seema’s story puts a devastating human face on the Post Office scandal, one of the gravest miscarriages of justice in modern British history. Bill Bailey pairs Seema with Jack, an artist of great emotional sensitivity, whose richly layered paintings explore, amongst other things, resilience. Will Jack's approach to Seema's portrait offer the perfect way to tell her story, full of friendships, family and strength?

programme.title: Beyond Paradise
programme.desc: An off-grid community is thrust into the spotlight when a land developer is terrorised by the image of a Green Man. As tensions rise, secrets surface that someone wants buried.

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Sue Perkins hosts, with guest panellists comedian Finlay Christie and journalist Camilla Long joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the news.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: It’s time for Conor and Jock to try the exciting Cork dating scene when two girls visiting the city catch their attention.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Channel Islands News
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather for the Channel Islands.

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Claudia looks back at some of the highlights from the series, featuring her studio audience and an array of star-studded sofa guests, including Lisa Kudrow, Jennifer Saunders, Niall Horan, Guz Khan, Jeff Goldblum, James McAvoy, Rita Wilson, Jamie Dornan, Anna Faris, Jimmy Carr, Ralph Fiennes, Rachel Zegler, Jamie Dornan and many more.

programme.title: Chaos Walking
programme.desc: 2257. A colony world community without women, where thoughts are heard aloud, is disrupted when teen Todd finds crash survivor Viola, and the pair must flee ruthless Mayor Prentiss.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Georgina Hayden and Richard Bainbridge, and by special guests Nish Kumar and the Great British Menu winner! Drinks expert Helen McGinn picks the wines to go with the studio dishes, and there are some great moments from the BBC food archive.

programme.title: Flaming Feasts
programme.desc: Chris explores the real Wrexham, from historic lager and five-generation fruit farms to vegan pioneers, Portuguese flavours and a fire-cooked feast celebrating local produce.

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: It’s FA Cup semi-final weekend. We preview Manchester City v Southampton and Chelsea v Leeds. Wayne Rooney meets Manchester United captain Bruno Fernandes, and we look ahead to a big weekend at the bottom of the Premier League table and Arsenal’s Women’s Champions League semi-final.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: This episode takes place in Shepton Mallet, Somerset, with Natasha Raskin Sharp at the helm, and the reds and blues led by John Cameron and Stephanie Connell. Which team will make the biggest profit or the smallest loss at auction? Meanwhile, Natasha tries her hand at making a famous local delicacy.

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan is at the Running Show to look ahead to the 46th London Marathon, which promises to set a new world record for the most participants in a marathon. She meets some of the inspirational charity runners, all with their own unique reasons to run this year, including those who are lacing up their trainers to raise millions of pounds for brilliant causes. And she is joined by former marathon world record holder Paula Radcliffe to look ahead to some of the most competitive elite races ever run.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 8 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, weather and features from the South West of England.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Live coverage from Wembley as Southampton take on Manchester City in the FA Cup semi-final. It’s 50 years since Southampton beat Manchester United to cause one of the biggest cup final upsets in history, and the Championship team produced another shock in the quarter-finals this year, when they knocked out Premier League leaders Arsenal. Manchester City have reached the semi-final stage for a record eighth consecutive year. Last year, they made it through to the final only to be beaten by Crystal Palace.

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: The legendary Blankety Blank is back! Bradley Walsh hosts as a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win some brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This week, he’s joined by Fred Sirieix, Laura Whitmore, Eddie Kadi, Josh Widdicombe, Vanessa Feltz and Scarlett Moffatt.

programme.title: The Weakest Link
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan is at the helm of one of the world’s most iconic quizzes. Eight celebrities are tested on their general knowledge as they compete to win up to £50,000 for their chosen charities. Round by round, one player will be voted the weakest link by their fellow contestants and will leave with nothing. Who will be crowned the strongest link and take home the prize for their charity? The celebrity contestants in this episode are Aisling Bea, La Voix, Ben Bailey Smith, Anna Richardson, Samantha Kinghorn, Will Best, Jason Byrne and Jacqui Joseph.

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Our heroes step up as a mystery virus sweeps across Holby.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee’s favourite band is Oasis, which is why he rushed online to buy tickets to see them in concert. A year on, he still can’t come to terms with the fact that he lost his place in the queue and missed out. Lucy suggests a night in watching Oasis on the TV instead, but Lee can’t get over this feeling he’s had for a long time... that it was all Lucy’s fault.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of six Premier League games including Arsenal against Newcastle, with Mikel Arteta’s side looking to return to the top of the table after being replaced by Manchester City in midweek. Tottenham face a crucial clash with Wolves at Molineux, while West Ham take on Everton at London Stadium. Fulham host Aston Villa, and Crystal Palace go to Anfield. There are also highlights of Sunderland versus Nottingham Forest at the Stadium of Light from Friday night.

programme.title: Wayne Rooney Meets Bruno Fernandes
programme.desc: Wayne Rooney catches up with Manchester United captain Bruno Fernandes.

programme.title: Candyman
programme.desc: For decades, residents of Chicago’s Cabrini-Green have heard stories of a supernatural killer known only as Candyman, allegedly the resurrected soul of a murdered slave wanting to exact vengeance on his killers. In the present day, visual artist Anthony McCoy (Yahya Abdul-Mateen II) and his girlfriend (Teyonah Parris) have moved into a luxury loft condo in a now gentrified Cabrini-Green. A chance encounter with a local old-timer (Colman Domingo) exposes Anthony to the true story behind Candyman and unleashes a wave of violence that puts him on a collision course with destiny.
